free_text: Mechanism of Formation and Decay of the Compound
Nuclei Gd-150 Produced by Two Entrance Channels
. Comparison with Theory, See
Add-Results.
5 to 7 Different Beam Energy Values were Obtained by
Tuning the Avf Cyclotron. Intermediate Energies were
Obtained by Thin Al-Degraders. Their Thicknesses,
Carefully Measured by Weighing and Alpha-Absorption,
Were Chosen As to Never Exceed an Energy Loss of More
Than 10%. Northcliffe and Schilling Tables (NUCL.DATA
TABLES A7,233(1970)) Were Used to Calculate the Energy
Loss.  The Beam Energy was Determined Very Precisely
Using Different Methods.
The Targets were Placed at the Bottom of a Faraday Cup.
At the Back of the Targets Al-Collectors were Placed
Thick Enough to Stop the Recoiling Nuclei.
Iradiation and Cooling Times are Unknown.
.Resolution 2.9 KeV.
From the Summation of All Individual Cross Sections
A Complete Fusion Cross Section is Calculated.
Complete Fusion Thresholds are Compared with
Calculated Fusion Barriers. In the Secondary
Reference a Systematic Comparison Between
Experimental Excitation Functions and Results of
Evaporation Calculations are Given.
No Information About the Cross Section
Errors is Given.  Errors Quoted in the Data Section are
In Most Cases Greater Than 15%.  The Significance of
These Errors is Uncertain.  The Heavy Ion Energy was
Known to Within 2% For the Individual Targets.
Nekw
Some Corrections have Been Included by
CAJAD
. Some Corrections For 'Reaction'. Converted
To Lower Case.
Last checking has been done.
